description: Done with or showing thought and attention; avoiding harm or mistakes. 'Thesa' names the quality of attentive caution—the awareness that prevents damage through foresight.
sound_symbolism: The word attends with care. 'The' /θe/ begins with gentle breath, attention present. Then 'sa' completes with steady awareness, vigilance maintained.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'thesa telo' (careful work). Related to 'kiro' (precise) but emphasizes avoiding harm rather than achieving accuracy.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Careful work was essential when materials were precious and replacement difficult—every action deserved full attention.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Careful attention is the foundation of mindfulness—the awareness that notices before acting.
